Name: indoximod
evidence_text: administration of lipid nanoparticles (LNPs)<sup>32</sup>. Conventional LNPs are composed of ionizable lipids, phospholipids, cholesterol and polyethylene glycol (PEG)-conjugated lipids<sup>4,33,34</sup>. While LNPs are highly effective for mRNA delivery, the loading efficiency of free small-molecule drugs in LNPs is low and risks drug leakage in vivo<sup>35-37</sup>. By contrast, prodrug-based nanosystems have higher drug loading capacity, good biocompatibility and controlled drug release in vivo<sup>38-41</sup>. Name: triethylamine
evidence_text: *Synthesis of Nitro-SS-Nitro*. 4-Nitrophenyl carbonochloridate (2.4 equiv.), triethylamine (3 equiv.) and 50 mL of THF were added into a reaction flask. Then 2-hydroxyethyl disulfide (1 equiv.) was dissolved in 50 mL THF and slowly added into the flask. The mixture was stirred at room temperature overnight. Upon completion, the reaction mixture was filtered to remove undissolved substance and collect filtrates. Name: methanol
evidence_text: *Synthesis of prodrug amine heads*. Amine head (3.0 equiv.), triethylamine (1.5 equiv.) and methanol were added into a reaction flask and stirred at 0 oC. Then 4-Nitro-SS-AA (1.0 equiv.) dissolved in methanol was slowly added into the flask. After stirring for 2 h, all solvents were removed by reduced pressure. The residues were further purified by preparative HPLC to afford prodrug amine heads for the following synthesis.
